title: Stress Trigger & Pattern Log
description: Track your stress triggers, your body's response, and what helped (or didn't) to identify patterns over time. Regular tracking builds the self-awareness needed to interrupt the stress escalation cycle early.
instructions: Complete this log once daily (ideally in the evening), or after a significant stress episode. Focus on noticing patterns rather than judging yourself.

fields.label: Overall stress level today (1 = very low, 10 = unbearable)
What was the main source of stress today?

options.label: Workload — too much to do, too little time
Control — lack of say over my work or schedule
Fairness — feeling treated unfairly or unrecognized
Values — doing things that conflict with what matters to me
Health — my own or someone I care about
Caregiving — demands from caring for others

fields.label: What specifically happened?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Had 3 back-to-back meetings then was asked to take on another project by end of week

fields.label: How did stress show up in your body?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Tight shoulders, headache, stomach tension, exhaustion, racing heart

fields.label: What did you do to cope?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Took a walk, vented to a friend, had a glass of wine, worked late, scrolled my phone

fields.label: How helpful was your coping? (1 = made it worse, 5 = neutral, 10 = very helpful)
Did you do anything restorative today? (Even something small counts)
fields.placeholder: e.g., 10-minute walk, called a friend, read for 20 minutes, nothing today
